content: To anyone experiencing this issue, as an alternative for iOS 14 use the `file_picker: 2.0.6` that already implements the new PhotosUI framework introduced with iOS 14.

@cpboyd - I'm trying your fork to see if I can get this working for a bug fix release. I'm using the git `dependencies` and`dependency_overrides`.

The iPad is on 12.3.1.

Let's only track this issue for the permission bug.
For people who is interested in the crash issue, please follow https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/66647, it has more detailed updates about the crash

@JZ-at-TP Hmm. I didn't really change much anything with the popover from @knaeckeKami and @orestesgaolin.

As that's the current work-around for avoiding the #66647 crash, I'll look into it when I have time.

> Updates:
> Found that the crash only happens when the image picker is full screen. So if we change https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/ios/Classes/FLTImagePickerPlugin.m#L71 to default or anything that's not full screen, for example: [UIModalPresentationPopover](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uimodalpresentationstyle/uimodalpresentationpopover), the crash won't happen. This workaround can be applied to prevent crash, the UI, however, will look a little different (I actually think the non-full screen one looks better):
> I haven't pin point the exact cause of modifying iOS layout engine on the background thread yet. It looks like a regression in iOS.

Thanks @cyanglaz..! It's worked. But it should be fixed for the full screen as well.

@lecicdjuro As promised, an update about @miguelpruivo's [file_picker]: it seems to be fixed with the latest version (`2.0.6`). In my case, it still doesn't work though: I see a "Picker Unavailable" window (which seems to be an iOS bug).

Regarding [image_picker], the latest version doesn't work either, but differently: I see a permission choice window (**which is not the case with [file_picker]**):
* if I select "Allow access to all photos" I see a blank screen then nothing (like a crash) and back as if I selected no photo
* If I select "Select Photos..." I see the same dismissible blank screen but no crash afterwards (I can dismiss it, then everything happens like I did not call the library).

Strange that in one case I see a permission window, and not in the other case. Strange iOS 14 new features...

> > Updates:
> > Found that the crash only happens when the image picker is full screen. So if we change https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/ios/Classes/FLTImagePickerPlugin.m#L71 to default or anything that's not full screen, for example: [UIModalPresentationPopover](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uimodalpresentationstyle/uimodalpresentationpopover), the crash won't happen. This workaround can be applied to prevent crash, the UI, however, will look a little different (I actually think the non-full screen one looks better):

I'm still experimenting crashes on iPad, even while accessing the gallery for the first time, if I set 
_imagePickerController.modalPresentationStyle = UIModalPresentationPopover;

No more problems instead with the iPhone simulator, when opening the gallery twice.

I tried to avoid setting _imagePickerController.modalPresentationStyle to any value, leaving it as default, as suggested, and everything seems to work with both the simulators

Thanks @cyanglaz!

Just as an update for anyone trying the modal, I tried implementing the `presentationControllerWillDismiss:`
https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins/commit/d91cbde30325695a59bd5c5414250164024b83c2

Unfortunately, I still seem to be able to trigger the race condition where `getImage()` never returns when rapidly dismissing the modal.

By moving the delegate assignment before the `presentViewController` it seems to have addressed **most** issues related to the plugin itself: https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins/commit/5e358faaa73fc1e45d8c1008746b97e21b4d0069

Try it with
```yaml
  image_picker:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins.git
      ref: test
      path: packages/image_picker/image_picker
```

@cyanglaz It seems like possibly more issues related to the overall Flutter engine and the simulator, though... Sometimes quickly dismissing the modal will result in certain Flutter widgets being unresponsive.

If I manage to close the widget containing the image_picker button, it seems that I can reopen it and then start the image_picker process... so maybe something with the rendering engine isn't properly handling the modal dismissal?

I have also been looking into this issue and trying to solve it and was wondering if we should simply switch to using the new [PhotoKit library](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/photokit?language=objc) when the image_picker plugin is run on iOS 14 or higher (see also the [WWDC 2020 - Meet the new Photos Picker](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2020/10652/) session)? 

The one problem I can see is that the new PhotoKit library doesn't support picking an asset directly from the camera (something that is supported by the `UIImagePickerController`). Meaning on iOS 14 the `source` parameter becomes obsolete.

I think I could implement this relatively easily but would be a waste of time if this is not the way to go. @cyanglaz, @cpboyd, how would you feel about this?
the option choose an image to access very inconvenience
@mvanbeusekom Thanks for the suggestions. At this moment, we don't have any concrete plan to integrate the plugin with the PhotoKit yet.

After offline discussion with @amirh, we decided to add a new flag in the [pickImage](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/lib/image_picker.dart#L55) api:

```
forceFullMetaData: defaults to true

Some platforms require less permissions for getting images without their full metadata,
when this flag is false the plugin fetches the image in a way that requires minimal extra permissions
from the platform (e.g on iOS the plugin won’t ask for the XXX permission).
When this flag is true the plugin tries to get the full image metadata which may prompt extra permission requests.
```

So on iOS, if you don't want the permission pop up to show on iOS 11 and above, after the fix, you can set this flag to `false`; however, you won't be able to get all the meta data of the image such as location, time etc.
On Android, this flag will not have any effect as android always requires permission and always returns meta data.

@mvanbeusekom What do you think about this?

@wenxiangjiang I'm still waiting for feedback on the most recent rebase

I believe that showing all photos isn't the issue for iOS 14+, since this is the expected behavior of the newer `PHPicker`. With this picker (which Image_picker already uses if iOS 14+), you don't need to request photos permission at all, the picker itself works as a permission prompt, that is why all photos are being shown even if you set the permission as selected photos. 

You can see more here: https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2020/10652/?time=70 

I believe the correct approach would be handling better the automatic permission prompt, and let iOS 14 users bypass it. At least, that was my workaround.
@victorbotamedi Yes, `UIImagePickerController` in iOS11 and above don't need to request photos permission too.

@victorbotamedi Yes, it'd be nice for those who want metadata to better handle if the user decides not to give permission.

My PRs are focused on enabling developers who don't need metadata to disable any possibility of permissions prompts on iOS.
@victorbotamedi could you please share details of your workaround?
over a year later?

Does the `image_picker` require photo library permissions on iOS for any of it's functionality for iOS 14 or later? For the specific use case of picking individual images, I know it is not required.

Can we remove the permission prompt for iOS >=14, or would that break other functionality of the plugin?

@jjoelson This is precisely the approach I ended up taking with my own app. You can use my branch by modifying your `pubspec.yaml`:

```yaml
  image_picker:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins.git
      ref: ios-no-permissions
      path: packages/image_picker/image_picker
```

You don't need the permissions in earlier versions of iOS either... It's only if you explicitly want the image metadata (like GPS coordinates, which I agree with Apple should be private)
